title: Electrical Signaling
definition: The process by which electrical signals are transmitted and processed within neurons, forming the basis of neural communication.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The way nerves communicate using electrical impulses, like a relay race where messages travel from one nerve to another.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The transmission and processing of electrical signals within neurons, underpinning the communication network of the nervous system.
difficulty_levels.expert: A fundamental principle in neuroscience that describes the propagation of electrical impulses along axons, enabling information transfer between neurons, and connecting to computer science through binary signaling and physics through electrochemistry.

extra_fields.insight: The all-or-nothing nature of action potentials that forms the bridge between biology and technology.
